text:
Title: Samsung galaxy ear buds PRO Review: Well. I really wanted to love these... but there a few things...
PROS
Super easy to connect to ALMOST any thing that has blue tooth
Sound is surprisingly good
They seem to charge quickly and keep a charge for a fair amount of time
The storage and charging box is small - but gets lost in the bottom of your purse
CONS
The tips they send with the ear buds are GARBAGE - in order to really use these you are going to need to invest in some memory foam type tips
The controls are spotty and sometimes don't work
The 'noise canceling' aspect doesn't really function - somewhat better with better foam tips
***********
I purchased these for travel - but haven't traveled with them yet. I hope I can find some good tips - apparently I need a larger memory foam tip than is out there. Eh.
Having said that, I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful. I feel that a 3 1/2 star rating would be better, I did generously give them 4. Time will tell.
cot: 1. Clues: Multiple PROS (easy connect, good sound, battery) and CONS (garbage tips, spotty controls, non-functional noise canceling). 2. Reasoning: Detailed balanced review with significant issues requiring additional purchase and core feature failures. However, the 4-star rating indicates overall satisfaction despite problems—suggesting the positives (sound quality, convenience) outweigh the negatives for the reviewer's use case. The 'really wanted to love' shows emotional investment in positive outcome. 3. Verdict: Positive sentiment with acknowledged flaws.

Title: Soft Fabric
Review: When I first opened this, I thought that there was no way that the XL would fit my size 16 body. However, it does stretch quite a bit and I was able to fit into it. It's not a flattering look by itself for someone my age, but I could definitely wear it underneath something. The description says it has a shelf support, but there's really no support at all. The fabric is soft. I would like something a little longer, but the length is adequate. I wouldn't want anything shorter.
cot: 1. Clues: Positive: 'does stretch quite a bit', 'able to fit', 'could definitely wear it', 'fabric is soft', 'length is adequate'. Negative: 'not a flattering look', 'no support at all', 'would like something a little longer'. 2. Reasoning: The review contains mixed assessments—functional but not ideal fit, comfortable fabric but misleading support claims, adequate but not preferred length. The balanced enumeration of likes and dislikes with 3-star rating indicates middling satisfaction. 3. Verdict: Neutral sentiment with offsetting satisfactory and unsatisfactory elements.

Title: Nice moisturizer, small jar, high price
Review: From a size perspective, this is a great little jar of moisturizer to keep in your pocket or purse to have with you at work, at the store or while traveling. The moisturizer is really nice, a little thick and takes a while to absorb, but afterwards my skin feels super soft. I am deducting a star, however, because of the price. I mean for $52 (the current price while I am writing this) is crazy high for what you get. I have lower priced moisturizers at home which work just as well , come in a much larger size and cost less. If it's only the portability you are looking at than this is a great little jar of moisturizer. Otherwise, shop around a little.
cot: 1. Clues: 'great little jar', 'really nice', 'super soft', explicit conditional recommendation vs 'crazy high', 'deducting a star', 'shop around'. 2. Reasoning: Strong product satisfaction with clear use case (portability) and effective results. The price objection is substantial ($52 noted as 'crazy') with explicit comparison to superior alternatives. The 'however' frames price as sole deduction reason. Conditional 'If it's only the portability' suggests narrow recommendation scope. 3. Verdict: Positive sentiment matches 4-star rating—good product with significant value concern.

text:
Title: A face wash where I must avoid the eye area is not for me; also it does not smell so good Review: When I selected this product, I originally thought this was a face cream (as in moisturizer). However, it's a cleanser.

This face wash is definitely not for me. A couple of times on the back of the tube it warns about avoiding the delicate eye area or any contact with the eyes. Yet it also says it is a gentle cleanser and effectively removes traces of makeup. I use makeup on my entire face, so a face wash that should not be used in the eye area is not very useful for me.

I did try this on the rest of my face and neck. A small amount of product (the size of half of a dime) lathers up very well. The wash feels very slick / slippery. I did not care for the smell. After rinsing off, I did feel cleansed and my skin felt soft. If this skin care cleanser was safe to use in the eye area, I would have rated this 4 stars (taking off points for smell and slickness). Since this is a face and neck cleanser, it must be okay to use on the entire face in my opinion. For that reason only, I dislike this skin care product and would never purchase it.
cot: 1. Clues: Fundamental mismatch with needs (eye area restriction), sensory dislike (smell), but acknowledges some positives (lathers well). 2. Reasoning: The core issue is product-need incompatibility rather than complete failure—user needs eye-safe cleanser, this isn't. Some functional competence (lathering) exists. The 'not for me' framing suggests personal mismatch rather than universal defect. 3. Verdict: Negative sentiment matching the 2-star rating, reflecting significant usability problems with minor functional redeeming qualities.

Some of them seem to be canvas or something similar. I think that some kitchen spills could easily seep through the fabric which defeats the purpose of wearing the apron in the first place. I was excited that VB has added some new products to their line, but I think this one needs to be reconsidered or at least tweaked.

text:
Title: Compact, Reliable
Review: Previously I was using another make of a 10-port hub and it worked okay, but it did not support USB 3.1, as this does.  Also, it had a very distracting strip of blue light showing that it was powered on.  This one has a tiny "pilot" light by each USB outlet, that lights up when that port is active.  I like that much better, as you can tell which ports are active.  Also everything plugs in vertically; my other hub had only 2 vertical ports and the remainder came out of 2 sides.  The side ports did make the cables a little neater, as they laid flat, whereas with this hub they stick straight up and don't look quite as nice.  The hub is compact, better than the older bulkier one that I was using.  You can never have too many USB ports and I am already using 8 of the 10 available, even though my computer is equipped with several also.  Just about every device these days is USB or wireless.  Nothing to do for set-up.  Just plug it in and use it. I needed a strong scissor to cut the top tip off. Do remember to do that before using because otherwise the liquid will spill out the sides during squeezing.

Title: My 3rd + 4th sets of TSLA leggins Review: I am 5'1" and 105lbs, 24" waist 33" hips, ordered an XS.

This fit more tightly than my other pair of leggings with a pocket at the waist rather than on the leg, kind of like a compression legging, but both styles are very comfortable. They smelled a little weird out of the bag so I threw them in the wash/dry and tried them on - good fit. They are more what I would call a mid rise, coming 2.5" below my true waist. Still a comfortable rise for me. I also own a pair of TSLA XS black fleece leggings. All four leggings are squat proof and pass a flash photo test (that is, you can't see my underwear if I take a photo with the flash). I keep coming back to this brand because it's so hard to find nice, affordable leggings especially in small sizes. I wore these leggings on two 1.5 mile long walks in the heat and stayed comfortable, so I would say they are moisture wicking and good to work out in. Whether or not they are good for runs probably depends on how they fit you as you really do not want to run in leggings if there is any chaffing at all or any other fit issue. They do have flat seams that are good for running.
cot: 1. text:
Title: Extremely Thick Cream Leaves Greasy Film Review: This Amazon Brand SOLIMO DRY SKIN CREAM (Fragrance free, Paraben free, Phthalate free, 16 Ounces) is an extremely thick cream that contains petrolatum , mineral oil, and lanolin, among other things. (Petrolatum is the main ingredient in both Vaseline and Aquaphor.) Because it’s so thick, the cream needs to be rubbed in--it isn’t easily absorbed into your skin.

After it’s rubbed in, the cream leaves a greasy film atop your skin. If you apply the cream to your hands, you will want to wipe off the residue. Otherwise you will transfer the film to keyboards, touchscreens, kitchen utensils, or anything else that you touch, for quite a while. If you apply the cream to your body, you will want to wear some sort of clothing over it to avoid transfer.

The cream is white, and totally fragrance free. The big 16-ounce jar will last a long time. I think that the product is best used as a night cream on dry feet, arms, or legs (covered by sleepwear). However, I prefer dry skin creams that are much more easily and quickly absorbed.
cot: 1. Clues: 'Extremely Thick', 'Greasy Film', 'isn't easily absorbed', 'want to wipe off the residue' - negative descriptors; however, the reviewer objectively explains ingredients and acknowledges some may find this acceptable (comparing to Vaseline/Aquaphor). 2. Reasoning: The review is descriptive and balanced, noting functional drawbacks (greasiness, residue) without emotional outrage or product failure claims. The 3-star rating suggests the product works as intended for some purposes but has significant usability issues. Mixed but not strongly negative. 3. Verdict: Neutral sentiment - the product has clear drawbacks but isn't condemned as useless.

text:
Title: Not flannel, are very large, cover your whole foot, but still ok.
Review: These ones came in a little too large for my taste I believe I ordered a Large but even if it was an XL the pants fit great at the top yet were really long all the way to the floor to the point my feet got stuck with them. They do not really feel like flannel if you ask me, and boy do I know about flannel because I've worn similar flannel pants all my life and always buy on winter whenever I get a chance, these are definitely not flannel. Overall they are ok I just need to make a few adjustments to the hem probably since it is a little too low/large but I was also a little disappointed that it's not soft flannel but rather feels more like cotton, so more stiff. The top part is ok though and eitherw ay they are probably more sturdy than flannel ones, although I personally prefer the latter because of the softness.
cot: 1. Clues: 'fit great at the top', 'ok', 'ok' (neutral-positive); 'too large', 'feet got stuck', 'definitely not flannel', 'disappointed', 'more stiff' (negative). 2. Reasoning: The review reveals multiple unmet expectations: sizing issues, material misrepresentation (claimed flannel, actually cotton), and stiffness. However, the product is wearable with adjustments ('need to make a few adjustments'), and the waist fit is good. The repeated 'ok' assessments and willingness to modify indicate acceptance rather than rejection. The 3-star rating reflects this middling position—disappointed by specific attributes but not returning the item. The expertise claim ('I know about flannel') adds weight to the material complaint but doesn't override the overall 'ok' verdict. 3. Verdict: Neutral sentiment due to functional adequacy despite significant expectation violations.

text:
Title: Rich in hyaluronic acid, noticeable imprvoement in my skin
Review: For the positives, this is a good moisturizing facial serum. Rich, absorbs quickly and contains hyaluronic acid (something both my dermatologist and my local spa technicians recommend greatly for younger looking skin). I have been using this now for almost a month and I do see a nice, one improvement in my complexion, skin looks glowing and hydrated. For the negatives, at close to $100 this is very expensive, you can purchase hyaluronic acid in other products at a substantially less cost. I also don't care for the fragrance, I find it a little overwhelming for me. Of course, given 5 minutes or so, when absorbed, the fragrance is basically gone. For the two negatives I am taking away a star in my review of this product.
cot: 1. Clues: 'good moisturizing', 'absorbs quickly', 'noticeable improvement', 'glowing and hydrated' (strong positives); 'very expensive', 'don't care for the fragrance' (negatives); explicit 4-star logic stated. 2. Reasoning: Significant efficacy benefits weighed against cost and minor sensory issue. The reviewer deliberately balanced positives against negatives to arrive at 4 stars, indicating strong satisfaction with caveats rather than reservation. 3. Verdict: Positive sentiment with minor deductions for value and preference.

text:
Title: Label Lists No Germ-Killing Ingredients; Not Made by Tea Tree Review: This ANTIBACTERIAL TEA TREE MINT HAND SOAP (with Spearmint, Jojoba and Olive Oil; Multipurpose Liquid Soap in Pump Dispenser; 16 oz) lathers reasonably well, but it does smell a lot like gasoline (as another reviewer noted). The pump is very difficult to open up.

This is NOT a Tea Tree brand product--it is a Buena Skin Cosmeceuticals product with a label that emphasizes just one of its many ingredients (tea tree leaf oil).

I think it is false advertising to label this hand soap as “antibacterial” soap (in the title of the product page). According to the label, the soap “helps wash away bacteria”. Well, so does water!

The ingredients are all “natural” ingredients. The listed ingredients do NOT include alcohol or any other germ-killing chemical. When you’re using this, you’re probably getting the same “antibacterial” effect that you get with ordinary soap and water. (My web research says that tea tree oil MAY be antibacterial when applied to the skin.)
cot: 1. Clues: The title immediately signals deception and missing claims. The review notes one minor positive (lathers well) but heavily emphasizes negative attributes: gasoline smell, difficult pump, brand misrepresentation, and false 'antibacterial' advertising claims. The sarcastic 'so does water' highlights strong dissatisfaction. 2. Reasoning: The reviewer frames this as deceptive marketing with multiple product failures. The single positive aspect is vastly outweighed by concerns about false advertising, unpleasant smell, and poor packaging design. The incomplete final sentence suggests frustration cut short. The core issue is perceived dishonesty from the seller, which typically generates strong negative sentiment. 3. Verdict: Strongly negative due to multiple serious complaints centered on misleading product claims.

Its also a bit confusing with the lines, and colors of the pants the model Is wearing!

This did not fit me snug like the back of what the model shows .

The neck line came up higher.
The arm holes where to big. The back Criss-Cross hung down open, although this idea is cute .
I think You have to have a Firm body to wear this, as everything in the back is open and shows.
In the front of the top, If you have a tummy it will show..
cot: 1. It also appears to be scentless. However, I absolutely hate the pump mechanism. Unless I hold the bottle at an angle, the pump makes a mess when dispensing the serum.

The current price of $44 is expensive for 3.4 ounces but this will last a while. The reason I rated this 3 stars is because of the dispensing pump and how pricey this is.
cot: 1. Clues: Positive: 'serum is great', 'moisturizes a lot', 'not greasy', 'scentless'. Negative: 'absolutely hate the pump mechanism', 'makes a mess', 'expensive'. text:

Review: This is a great, lightweight full coverage foundation. Goes on smoothly and does not look cakey or flakey on my skin. I am typically fussy when it comes to my facial products, so I was skeptical when I first ordered this. However, I was pleasantly surprised to see how flawless this product worked for me. It smoothes my skin, minimizing poors and making me appear to not be wearing foundation (but my face looks amazing). I had never heard of DermaBlend before I ordered this, but I am definitely sold. This will be my foundation of choice going forward. Absolutely love how it makes my skin look and feel.

text:
Title: Do not buy!
Review: This is the second Sony BDP-S5100 3D Blu-ray Disc Player I have owned. The first one was refurbished and stopped accessing any internet content after about 6 months. Amazon was very nice and replaced it for me even though it was well past their 30 day refund policy. The second one was supposedly brand new but came shipped in a plain brown box. Once again it worked great at first but here I am only 3 months later and having the same problem. Says I have no problems with my network connection but won't let me do anything that requires internet, so no Netflix or Amazon video, which is the whole reason I bought a new Blu-ray player in the first place.
cot: 1. Clues: 'Do not buy!', 'second' unit with same problem, 'stopped accessing any internet content', 'same problem', 'only 3 months later', 'whole reason I bought' undermined. 2. Reasoning: Recurring defect across two units indicates systematic product flaw. The core functionality (internet streaming) failed in both cases. Amazon's good service noted but doesn't compensate for defective product. The purpose of purchase completely defeated. Pattern of failure suggests design or manufacturing defect. 3. Verdict: Strongly negative—repeated failure of essential function.

This does detangle but sort of rips at my hair. It is best used in the shower. The comb has a wide tooth and is okay but is also cheap. The set is pretty but it is not worth the price. If this were $4 or less, it would have received 3 stars as just an okay set for occasional use. At its current price, I would say pass. Note that fingerprints show up easily on the faceplate of the brush.
cot: 1. Clues: 'outrageous', 'feels cheap', 'dollar store plastic', 'rips at my hair', 'not worth the price', 'I would say pass' - strongly negative; 'does detangle', 'okay', 'pretty' - minor positives overwhelmed by criticism. 2. text:
Title: Holds the edges, with an added bonus (insert sarcasm here) Review: I'm a 3c curly with some gray. I wanted to sleek back my hair in a ponytail to highlight the silvers but keep them tame. My hair length is mid-back with major shrinkage, really fine, and prone to frizz.

I followed the directions - this product did it - sleeked my hair and I thought I was fly.

Until the flakes came like cradle cap! This caused *major, major* deeply embedded flakes all at the hairline. Once I stopped using this and applied a scalp treatment to the hairline, poof! Flakes gone.

What a waste. I couldn't return as it's outside the 30 day window period.
cot: 1. Clues: 'insert sarcasm here', 'Until the flakes came like cradle cap', '*major, major* deeply embedded flakes', 'What a waste', 'couldn't return'. 2. Reasoning: Initial success ('sleeked my hair', 'thought I was fly') dramatically overturned by severe adverse effect. The cradle cap comparison emphasizes severity and unpleasantness. Causation clearly established (flakes disappeared after discontinuation). Financial loss emphasized ('What a waste', unable to return). The sarcastic 'added bonus' framing underscores betrayal of expectations. 3. Verdict: Strongly negative sentiment.

text:
Title: Wonderful product! Highly recommend!
Review: I need to begin by saying I absolutely love Ahava products. I found Ahava on a pilgrimage I did to Israel in 2008 and a visit to the site of Masada at the Dead Sea. I picked up a gift package of a few of their products and I was immediately hooked. Every day I use their Mud exfoliator, at night I use their night cream and at least once a week I use one of their Mud Pack facial products. I am 61 years old and am often told I look to be in my low 50s. I was thrilled when I saw this new Ahava Osmoter product. I have been using this daily now for about a week and I will say my skin feels and looks like it is glowing. It goes on like silk, has a nice subtle fragrnace and my skin feels amazing throughout the day (even after I put on makeup and/or sun screen). I know this is pricy and you might be able to do better purchasing elsewhere (if you find it cheaper). You won't find a product line that is better for you than Ahava, feeding your skin richly with Dead Sea minerals and keeping you looking young and vibrant.
cot: 1. Clues: 'Wonderful product!', 'Highly recommend!', 'absolutely love', 'immediately hooked', 'thrilled', 'glowing', 'like silk', 'amazing'. 2. Reasoning: The review contains exclusively positive language with superlatives, brand loyalty evidence, and concrete benefits (appearance of youth, skin texture). The 5-star rating aligns perfectly with the enthusiastic, detailed praise spanning years of brand experience. No negative elements present. 3. Verdict: Strongly positive with high confidence.

text:
Title: Unique design is helpful for a good grip; clippers are not as sharp as my regular ones Review: There are two clippers and a tiny carry satchel in this set. The clippers can be used like regular clippers in the straight position or the head can be angled. I tried them on my toenails and it did take some getting used to the configuration to cut my nails. What I do love is that I have a better grip on the clipper due to the angle. However, I do not feel the cutting edge is sharp enough. My regular nail clippers are sharper or at least work like they feel sharper.

The current price of $14.99 comes to $7.50 per clipper.
cot: 1. Clues: 'What I do love' about grip; 'However' introduces sharpness concern; price mentioned neutrally without judgment. 2. Reasoning: Clear pros (better grip, unique design) and cons (not sharp enough, learning curve). The reviewer explicitly weighs both sides without strong emotional investment. The price note suggests value consideration without conclusion. This balanced evaluation fits neutral sentiment. 3. Verdict: Neutral sentiment with mixed but moderate opinions, matching the 3-star rating.

I highly recommend this for gift giving!
cot: 1. Clues: 'Great gift', 'Really nice', 'Nice presentation', 'looks great', 'cute little writing', 'absolutely loved it', 'highly recommend', 5-star rating. 2. Reasoning: Overwhelmingly positive language throughout with no negative elements. Multiple superlatives and explicit recommendation confirm strong satisfaction. So, if you have ity bity feet go for it.

The enthusiastic recommendation for appropriate foot size indicates the product is good for its intended audience. 3. Fits as expected. Only problem, the top sole is a little thin. I hit it on my bed frame & punctured a hole in them. Im so heartbroken
cot: 1. Clues: 'perfect', 'love them', 'Very comfy', 'Fits as expected', enthusiasm vs 'thin', 'punctured', 'heartbroken', extended search context. 2. Title: Great between pedicures to keep your feet looking beautiful!
Review: This is actually one of the best things I have ever gotten from Amazon. First, I go to get pedicures at least 2-3 times a month. I have awfully rough heels and the balls of my feet that the pedicure people try to address. And, although my feet feel pretty good and smooth for a few days after my pedicure, it isn't long before the bottoms of my feet are rough, scaly and peeling again. In comes this Denicer foot file. I am now using this once or twice a week between pedicures. It's a quick and easy procedure and the results are amazing. My feet stay soft and smooth all the time now (not just a couple of days a month) and they look, and feel healthier. I am thrilled to have found this product and I know there are other brands out there. I don't know if other systems could possibly be better (as I have never tried any others), but this one is great. It is powerful and it works. I highly recommend this to anyone who has rough and scaly feet like me.

Review: I used to wear MAC Goldfinch, until they discontinued it...it's the closest I've found to theirs. I pair it with my lipstick & it creates a wonderful, shimmering lip. Not only is it a great color, but it also has staying power & moisturizes the lip. I highly recommend!
cot: 1. Clues: 'closest I've found', 'wonderful, shimmering lip', 'great color', 'staying power', 'moisturizes', 'highly recommend'. 2. Reasoning: The user found a replacement for a beloved discontinued product, expressing satisfaction across multiple dimensions (color, performance, comfort). Strong recommendation language indicates enthusiasm. 3. Verdict: Strongly positive sentiment matching the 5-star rating.

Title: Best skin cleanser on the market today!
Review: This Revision Skincare Papaya cleanser is my new favorite addition to my daily beauty regimen. A little goes a long way and my skin feels amazingly clean, soft, smooth and doesn't leave my skin dry after using it. I am a 62 year old female who is very active and after I visit the gym or play a game of tennis, this is my go-to facial wash. I also really love the fragrance of this as well, leaves my feeling fresh and revitalized. I highly recommend this for all skin types and ages.
cot: 1. Clues: 'Best', 'new favorite', 'amazingly clean, soft, smooth', 'go-to', 'really love', 'fresh and revitalized', 'highly recommend', 'all skin types and ages'. 2. Reasoning: Superlative language throughout with specific functional benefits validated across multiple use contexts (daily, post-exercise). Title: Larger brush is more effective for me.
Review: I admit to not being a make-up pro or connoisseur of brushes, which might affect my review. For my basic, everyday needs, though, I found the larger of these brushes to be quite nice. It's a great texture/tightness and super effective in applying powder. The smaller brush is much looser in texture and didn't seem to pick up as much product (highlighter), or be quite what I was looking for in applying the product. The smaller brush is not the "must have" for me, but the larger brush is a keeper.
cot: 1. Clues: 'quite nice', 'great texture', 'super effective', 'keeper' (positives for large brush); 'much looser', 'didn't seem to pick up', 'not the must have' (negatives for small brush). 2. text:
Title: Lovely lavender coconut fragrance; glides on smooth; protection only lasted me 6 hours Review: There are three 2-ounce solid tubes in this package. The deodorant glides on smooth and  it feels moisturizing. Unfortunately, since this does not have aluminum (which I require), I stink after six hours. The fact that it even lasted six hours is amazing because other non-aluminum deodorants last me only two to four hours. The results are two stars for me. I rated it three stars because I know for a fact that no product w/o aluminum has ever worked 24 or 48 hours for me. They claim this works 48 hours but that might only be for a few people. Personally, I take a shower every 12 hours, so I do not need such long-length protection. I do love the coconut lavender fragrance, however. 

Note that the stick deodorant has to be pushed up from the bottom to expose the balm. This means if too much is exposed, the cap has to be used to squish it back down into the tube. I would have preferred a container that has a turn dial instead.

At the current price of $12.72 ($14.97 minus 15%), this comes to an expensive $4.24 per 2-ounce tube.
cot: 1. Clues: 'Lovely', 'glides on smooth', 'feels moisturizing', 'love the fragrance' (positives); 'stink after six hours', 'results are two stars' (negatives); explicit 3-star reasoning. 2. Reasoning: Thoughtful balanced review—acknowledges product performs relatively well for its category but fails personal needs; appreciates formulation while recognizing limitation; explicit rating justification shows fair assessment. 3. Verdict: Neutral sentiment matching 3-star rating—good product, wrong fit for reviewer.

I also get microneedling occasionally from the dermatolgist and my skin burns. This aloe gel works great for both, my skin feels cool and pain is diminished. I love the lavender fragrance in this gel as well as it not only cools, refreshes and moisturizes my skin but also leaves my skin feeling and smelling great. Highly recommend!

text:
Title: Failed after 4 months Review: My Samsung 32Gb EVO Class 10 Micro SDHC card (genuine) failed in my Samsung Galaxy Tab Pro 8.4 after 4 months. Lost all data, could not reformat. It's a nice little orange and white wafer to look at this point, nothing more. I'm going to try a 64GB MicroSDXC next. Wish me luck!

Update 7/9/2016 I sent this in under Samsung warranty and received a new replacement which has been working fine so far, but I'm not using it for critical data. So I upped my rating from one star to two stars for honoring the warranty.
cot: 1. Clues: 'Failed after 4 months', 'Lost all data', 'could not reformat', 'nothing more' (severe negative); 'honoring the warranty', 'working fine so far', 'upped my rating from one star to two stars' (partial recovery). 2. Reasoning: The update significantly complicates sentiment. Initial experience was catastrophic (data loss, total failure). Warranty service provided functional replacement, creating partial satisfaction. However, the 'not using it for critical data' caveat indicates persistent trust damage. The one-to-two-star upgrade confirms marginal improvement, not genuine satisfaction. The brand retention (staying with Samsung, trying larger capacity) suggests pragmatic rather than enthusiastic continuation. 3. Verdict: Negative sentiment with partial mitigation through customer service recovery, but fundamental product trust not restored.

Title: Easiest way to track your activity ... and it syncs!
Review: I chose the Fitbit One to better record my activity (as an alternate to a pedometer which had iffy accuracy.)  Now that I've had Fitbit One for a few days I must say that I totally love it!  It is small, clips securely to your clothing, the displays are great (including its cheery greetings!) and it automatically downloads info into Myfitnesspal or other diet tracking sites.  Couldn't be easier!  The Fitbit site is great and very useful.  This unit even tracks your sleep patterns!  Would recommend this item to anyone seeking to track and improve their activity levels
cot: 1. Title: The results do not wow me; my hair was soft but lifeless after using this Review: This review is for the Pantene Nourishing Oil Split End Serum.

I applied a couple of squirts to the ends of my short to medium fine curly hair and worked my way up. My hair dried soft but was completely lifeless after using this. I also cannot determine if it helps split ends because I cannot find any on me. However, I love the fragrance. It smells like apple to me. Nevertheless, I need to be wow'd by more than just the scent. It is okay but nothing spectacular.

At the current price of $12.59 (after applying the $1 coupon offer), this comes to $4.20 a bottle which is on the high side.
cot: 1. Clues: Disappointed title, one positive (soft, love fragrance) vs. multiple negatives (lifeless, cannot determine efficacy, need to be wow'd, nothing spectacular, price concern), explicit okay assessment. 2. Reasoning: The product delivers minimal benefit (softness, scent) but fails on primary claims (split end repair, vitality) and value. The cannot determine efficacy suggests possible non-need rather than failure, complicating assessment. The explicit okay and nothing spectacular align with middle-ground sentiment. 3. Verdict: Neutral sentiment reflecting adequate but underwhelming performance with unverified primary claims.

The fit of this garment was extremely disappointing.

text:
Title: Cheery color; brush heads are soft w/no shedding; some are too stiff Review: There are 10 make-up brushes in this set. The bristles are soft and there is absolutely no shedding.

Their soft bristles feel great against my skin and work like many other brushes I tried previously.What  do not like is that two of the large brushes feel like paint brushes because they are tightly compressed together. I prefer the one large brush that flows with my strokes. It is the same with one of the small brushes. The handles are okay but not as easy to keep a grip on as brushes with an hourglass type handle. These are definitely functional and there is a brush for almost any task but there is no lip brush.

The current price of $14.39 is cones to a slightly pricey $1.44 per brush. This set is not my first choice in brushes. The Daubigny 16Pcs Complete Green Premium Synthetic Makeup Brush Set (Amazon Item #B08PFQQ9FP) is much better and is cheaper per brush.
cot: 1. text:
Title: Flattering bathing suit, crazy design.. Review: I was a little taken back by this bright sunset orange color, and Hawaii black big palm trees beach design all over. Its not the white
Back round it shows here.
I could see this design more on board shorts! The sleeves are black, as is the neck , and zipper.
The cups are sewn in, and one side has a big indentation in the cup, other side is ok.
You do not have to have this all the way unzipped, as the zipper locks in place good higher up.

I Can’t imagine what kind of tan or sunburn you would get from this bathing suit that comes up to the neck and has sleeves!
I live in California at the beach. I realize the sun all the time on our skin is not our friend, but this is strange.

I wear a size 10, and I’m 5’2. I think a medium instead of a large might of fit me better, but this L I can wear. The back was a bit big, or for a taller person. The tush looks cute.
This does NOT come up real high on the sides to almost your waist, and it is a flattering bathing suit.

Title: Spacious wallet w/9 card slots & a double billfold but this anthracite black shows all scratches Review: This is a tri-fold wallet with a center ID window. On the left and right folds there is room for four credit cards on each side if counting the slot behind each bank of three slots and a center ID window. There is also adouble billfold.

The front of the wallet has a nice lion's head embossing on it.

The wallet also comes with RFID protection to protect the credit cards fromscanners. However, I have no way of verifying if this works or not.

It also comes in an attractive giftable box.

In summary, the slots in this wallet allow for the easy retrieval of all cards. What I hate about this wallet is that the anthracite black color shows all the fingerprints and scratches as the surface making all imperfections visible. The leather is stiff but also on the thin side. I feel any price more than $10 for this version of the wallet is too much.
cot: 1. Title: Too bland of a fragrance for my diffusers & I don't use it for other purposes Review: For .33 fluid ounces, the current price of $18.99 is very expensive for this. However, based on the description, this is harvested from trees that grow in the South American rainforest. I'm not sure if it actually comes from the Amazon or if Amazon trees are grown elsewhere and it is harvested in another location. Nevertheless, the description said it was sweet smelling. Therefore, I ordered it thinking it would be perfect for my diffuser. After trying this, it is very bland and has almost no scent worthy of sniffing.

Per the Amazon description, this type of essential oil "is a powerful anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antibacterial. The unique aroma and properties of this terpene can be utilized as a food additive, in cosmetics, creams, toothpaste, and other commercial products."  I suppose if one is using it for those purposes, it would be worthy of a higher rating. Unfortunately, I am disappointed as the statement "Copaiba Oil has a friendly, sweet aroma that encourages easy breathing" does not hold true for me.
cot: 1. Clues: 'very expensive', 'not sure if it actually comes from the Amazon' (skepticism), 'bland', 'almost no scent worthy of sniffing', failed expectation ('thinking it would be perfect'). 2. Reasoning: Price-value mismatch is primary concern. Product failed core function (fragrance for diffuser). Skepticism about sourcing authenticity adds distrust. It took a long time (about 10 minutes) for the salts to dissolve. My hands came out feeling soft.

The only difference to me between this and Epsom salt is that this has a fragrance and typical Epsom salts do not. However, some of today's Epsom salts have fragrances but they are not as good as this.

The current price of $28 is extremely pricey for this especially when there are probably only four servings in this container. Since I cannot stand the fragrance, I would not purchase this no matter how cheap it was. I am never using this one again.

I use these to clean my cat's urine off the walls of the litter pan but I wet the wipe first. They can be used to remove make-up with soap and water.

These are expensive. At the current price of $9.90 it comes to $.14 cents per wipe, which is about $.10 to $.12 cents more than wet baby wipes can be purchased for. Also a big negative to me is that there is no perforation in the package. Instead I have to cut the side open to extract the wipes.

Title: Manicure bench is narrow, tall, slippery, & it smells Review: At the current price of $15.99, this manicure bench is more reasonably priced than a competitor with the same bench. Since the bench is narrow on top, the person leaning their hands may have a tendency to tip this over. In addition, there are no rubber feet on the "legs." As a result, it is necessary to put a towel under this bench to keep it from slipping when placed on a solid non-stick surface.

Other than this, this bench is very nice. If it were lower to the ground, about two inches wider, and had rubberized feet on the bottom, this would have received 5 stars. In its current state it is functional but not the best it could be. The fact that it smells is also bothersome to me. I suppose it could double as a child's pretend ironing board.
cot: 1. Clues: 'reasonably priced', 'quality...pretty good', 'very nice' vs 'narrow, tall, slippery', 'smells', 'functional but' [incomplete]. 2. Reasoning: The reviewer explicitly states this would be 5 stars with design improvements, but currently finds it merely functional. Mixed positive and negative elements balance out to a middle-ground assessment. The 3-star rating confirms ambivalence. 3. Verdict: Neutral sentiment with balanced pros and cons.

I believe this product is a quality product similar to Latisse, but I don’t think it’s for me. You should definitely do some research before using and decide if the benefits are worth the risk.

Title: This is a bare bones basic curling iron; good for tight curls on very short hair Review: This review is for the 9mm Curling Iron-Pink.

I have short to medium fine curly hair and I can get even a more tighter curl with this iron. However, it is a bare bones model. There is a light telling the user when it has reached a certain temperature (meaning it stopped blinking); however, it is hard to see because it is the same color as the iron (reddish pink). There's a loosey goosey on/off switch on the one side and a sticky temperature control that goes from 80 to 230 degrees. Nevertheless, this all works.

The kick-stand is good as is the swivel cord ability.

This makes my curls tighter because of the small barrel. If one is looking for a tight curl and wants a super basic curling iron, then this is it.
cot: 1. Clues: Positive: 'good for tight curls,' 'can get even a more tighter curl,' 'this all works,' 'kick-stand is good,' 'swivel cord ability' good, functional result achieved. Reasoning: The reviewer accepts trade-offs: basic build quality and usability issues in exchange for functional performance at achieving tight curls. The 'Nevertheless, this all works' is key—criticisms are cosmetic/ergonomic, not functional. The 3-star rating reflects 'you get what you pay for' neutrality: it works for specific needs (tight curls, short hair) but lacks refinement. 3. Verdict: Neutral sentiment with functional satisfaction balanced against quality compromises.

Title: jumbo clear cosmetic zippered case
Review: I purchased this to hold a bunch of velcro like rollers I use on occasion.  It's perfect for that as it is clear and you can see what's in it!  The zipper is a bit of a challenge. Partly because the case comes folded flat.  I recommend you heat for just a few seconds in your dryer (I have a dryer closet so it didn't tumble - but warmed up) and so I could more easily unfold it without damaging the bag.  The zipper had a really big tongue on it - thank goodness - other wise it might impossible to zip.  The square top is another part of the challenge to the zipper because it's a sharp edge on both ends - requiring both hands at the very least.  They could have made the zipper just open the top, and not go down the sides, solving the zipper challenge, BUT it wouldn't open as wide.  Eh. That is kind of a bummer.  But it isn't fatal to me - just annoying - lost a star for that.  It is plastic and so it could become torn or pierced - I recommend light use.  The handles are a woven cloth like feel to them and the handles are very long. Works for me.
cot: 1. Clues: 'perfect for that', 'clear and you can see what's in it' vs extensive zipper difficulties requiring workarounds. 2. Reasoning: The 4-star rating acknowledges the product serves its core purpose well despite significant design flaws in the zipper. The reviewer found solutions but recognizes poor engineering. 3. Verdict: Positive sentiment with notable reservation matching the 4-star rating.

text:
Title: Probably great for a professional
Review: I was in the market for a new camera and when I saw this bag in the Vine program I thought I would snap it up. I did get my Canon Rebel (thanks Amazon!) and several lenses to boot (in preparation for my trip this month to Israel). Although the quality of the bag is good, the size to me is really inconvenient. First, it is shaped like a back pack, which makes it kind of big. I am not sure if I am going to be able to bring this on the plane with me (as the boarding guidelines state 1 carry-on and I have a purse and laptop I am bringing). In addition, there are straps and zippered compartments which are not really conveniently placed. It's not an easy bag to get your gear in and out of. I see this bag as a great tool to have if you are backpacking as you have a place to hang a tripod and there is a small storage area if you wanted to bring a snack with you. Title: Disappointing product
Review: As a long-time Kindle/ Kindle Fire user, I had expected the Fire TV Stick with Voice Remote to function as well as the Kindles I have owned (I am on my third).  Unfortunately, the Fire TV Stick has been a major disappointment.  Multiple apps do not work or will not load at various times (Netflix has never worked).  The Fire TV Stick seems to overheat every single time I use it and I end up having to unplug it and wait a few hours (or overnight) for it to work correctly again.  It usually overheats within about two to three episodes of a tv show (approximately 2 to 2.5 hours of use).  The home screen is always slow to load (and sometimes freezes, which means I have to restart the Fire Stick).  The only reason I have kept the Fire Stick is for Amazon music streaming, so the $34.99 cost seems okay to me (I bought the Fire Stick on sale during Amazon's Prime Day 2016).  I know my internet connection is not the issue because I can stream the same shows on my Kindle Fire at the same time, and it works without any issues.
cot: 1. Clues: 'major disappointment', 'apps do not work', 'overheat every single time', 'slow to load', 'freezes' (strongly negative); 'only reason I have kept' (weak retention signal). 2. Reasoning: Despite overwhelmingly negative technical experiences, the user explicitly states they have kept the device for a specific limited purpose (Amazon music). This retention indicates the product retains marginal value for their specific use case, preventing a fully negative rating. The 3-star rating suggests the reviewer distinguishes between complete unusability and severely compromised functionality. 3. Verdict: Neutral sentiment reflecting severely degraded but not entirely worthless product experience.

The card this comes on is thin, but is decorated in light color printed on flowers, and in the middle of it,
It says, Girls Rule in black.

This came in a plain lite brown box with a Amazon plain sticker, that does not look gift giving to me. Inside the box in plastic is the necklace. If this is a gift plan on rewrapping this.

I took a star off for the packaging, and another off cause the pendant is Ok, but any young girl that loves frozen would be
Happy to get this.
How long this will last is a mystery to me!

Title: Not Ideal for Grinding Natural Nails Review: This PROFESSIONAL ELECTRIC NAIL DRILL (35000RPM, with 6 Drills for Acrylic Gel Nails, Home Salon Use) has both a manual control and a foot pedal control. As the product page states, using it takes some practice. It’s really intended for grinding acrylic nails, and for other serious grinding applications.

It was my intention to use the device to grind natural nails (in particular, one toenail that has nail fungus, that would benefit from thinning). However, the machine runs so fast, and my hand/eye coordination is so poor, I’m afraid to use it. For me, ordering this sophisticated grinder for use on natural nails was a mistake.
cot: 1. I cannot use the hole for the penis because i move around at work my part comes out of the pouch into the underwear. Also it's not easy to remove the front pouch when you go to urinate. I do not recommend it for someone that is trying to separate the privates. The bag for the testis is too small and the hole for the penis is not close to the body.

When I think about my favorite face products, I tend to like ones that have amazing natural scents. I'm in my early 50's and my skin is fairly sensitive. It didn't irritate at all. I'm also dealing with "maskne" from frequent mask wearing and it does seem to help with that, or at least it doesn't aggravate my skin. Overall, I found this to be okay.

It almost takes all night to absorb in well or gets removed via the pillowcase; I'm not sure. Also, one night I made the mistake of rubbing my eyes and it does burn slightly. Although I will finish this cream, I would never buy this again because it is not to my liking despite its moisturizing effects.

Title: Manicure bench is narrow, tall, slippery, & it smells Review: At the current price of $17.99, this manicure bench is more reasonably priced than a competitor with the same bench. The quality of the workmanship is pretty good but the legs elevate this bench about 5.25" off the ground. Since the bench is narrow on top, the person leaning their hands may have a tendency to tip this over. In addition, there are no rubber feet on the "legs." As a result, it is necessary to put a towel under this bench to keep it from slipping when placed on a solid non-stick surface.

Other than this, this bench is very nice. If it were lower to the ground, about two inches wider, and had rubberized feet on the bottom, this would have received 5 stars. In its current state it is functional but not the best it could be. The fact that it smells is also bothersome to me. I suppose it could double as a child's pretend ironing board.
cot: 1. Clues: 'reasonably priced', 'workmanship pretty good', 'very nice' vs 'narrow, tall, slippery', 'tendency to tip', 'necessary to put a towel', 'functional but'. 2. Reasoning: Clear design flaws requiring workarounds, yet fundamentally usable and well-made. Verdict: Neutral—functional with notable but not fatal compromises.

The bristles are soft but they feel nylon-like which is not as good as brushes that feel like real hair. Also, the brush heads and case have a slight chemical smell. The magnetic closure case is super attractive, however.

This is a decent travel set for someone who is starting their make-up journey. The current price of $11.51 ($12.79 minus 10%) is competitively priced for this set as it comes to $1.64 per brush.

Title: Messy. Review: I have use this twice, and I find it to be a mess!
When I took the Gel stick out of the tube it’s so thick there was slime hanging on it.
I Put it back in the tube, and when I took it out again I had to keep pushing
The stick/wand against the side to wipe it off, it was better, then put it on
My top eyelashes. Waited for it to dry a bit, then used the fiber wand it was not such a mess. Using the fiber wand I waited till it was almost dries before using more coats. I also used a clean eye-lash brush so It would not look so thick and clumpy on my eye lashes.
I use a good mascara but I’m more into a more natural look, then hard look, as that is what this looks like to me.
I have to say, I did not get black under my eyes from this, just a bit itchy.
cot: 1. Clues: Negative: 'mess,' 'slime,' 'thick and clumpy,' 'hard look' vs preferred natural look. Positive: 'did not get black under my eyes,' found workarounds that improved results. 2. text:
Title: This is a tricky straightener to use but it works; best on long hair Review: I have short to medium fine curly hair and this straightener is difficult to use on that length hair. It is best for longer hair because one has to get the strands to seat between the tines in the the ironing comb.

I used on dry hair (hair needs to be dry, per the instructions). I then set this to about 280 degrees. There is no degree setting that is readable on the handle. It only has a marker for the lowest setting at 265 degrees and the highest setting at 365 degrees Fahrenheit (or 130 to 200 Celsuis).

Once the light stopped blinking to let me know the temperature had been reached, I tried to use this like a comb to comb it through my hair. That DOES NOT WORK as it does not grip the hair. Instead I had to thread my hair into the comb by pulling it away from my scalp and laying the comb over my strands to get it between the tines. Once I did that, the straightening power was great.

If it were not so tricky to use, I would highly recommend this. However, it does take some getting use to.
cot: 1. Clues: 'tricky', 'difficult to use', 'DOES NOT WORK' for user's method vs. 'but it works', 'best on long hair', detailed usage attempt - usability issues with acknowledged functionality. 2. Reasoning: The reviewer invested significant effort testing the product and provides technical feedback. While they couldn't make it work for their hair type/length, they don't claim it's defective—just mismatched to their needs. The 'but it works' concession and specification that it's 'best on long hair' shows the product has merit for others. The incomplete sentence suggests frustration but not rage. 3. Verdict: Frustrated but objective neutral sentiment recognizing product-potential mismatch.

Title: soap pumps
Review: I purchased these to use with my shampoo and conditioner.  First, forget the twist on funnel as it's just easier to pour or squeeze your product into the bottle.  Then, the pump action... the conditioner actually comes out fairly well.  But the shampoo - you have to pump down and pull up the nozzle to get a tiny bit out.  Hmmm.. * note - I tried several different types of shampoo and even hand soap.  I really wanted to be able to quick pump a couple of squirts of shampoo one handed... that is not going to happen with these.  The funnel can be used with thinner fluids and is hard sided... so you need to be able to screw it on to a bottle you're planning to use or hold it.  It's got a pretty good opening- about 3/4" eyeballing it. The bottles are clear plastic, as are the pumps - with metal inner spring.  All it all I am going to use what I have but I am going to keep looking for an effective pump action bottle.  I may have expected too much out of these.  I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful.  Hope this helps!
cot: 1. Clues: Positive: 'conditioner actually comes out fairly well', 'pretty good opening'. Negative: 'forget the twist on funnel', 'have to pump down and pull up', 'not going to happen', wanted functionality failed. 2. Reasoning: Mixed functionality - works for one intended use (conditioner) but fails for another (shampoo, one-handed use). The detailed troubleshooting suggests the reviewer found partial value but overall disappointment. 3. Verdict: Neutral sentiment with mixed functionality results, matching the 3-star rating.

text:
Title: This fragrance is too intense for my senses Review: I love trying new fragrances and often buy them at TJX, Marshalls, and Home Goods without knowing what they smell like. I usually choose the high-end names. I purchased a 1.6 ounce bottle for $24.99 at Marshalls.

The scent is strong and smells like something from the 1920's (like my grandmother wore). For me its okay but not a scent I would buy again simply because it is too intense.
cot: 1. Clues: 'love trying new fragrances', 'okay', 'not a scent I would buy again', 'too intense' - personal mismatch rather than quality failure. 2. Reasoning: Reviewer is experienced fragrance enthusiast who appreciates exploration. Product quality isn't questioned—scent is just too strong for personal preference. 'Okay' indicates tolerable but not enjoyable. No anger or disappointment in quality, just sensory incompatibility. Polite rejection. 3. Verdict: Neutral sentiment matching 3-star rating, acknowledging acceptable quality with personal unsuitability.

text:
Title: fast dry nail treatment
Review: This works fairly well... but maybe not quite as fast as advertised.  That being said, it does make it easier to move on than without.  I like it - will continue to use it.  Super easy.  Paint your nails - let that dry at least a couple of minutes.  Then use the dropper - and drop from your cuticle to the tip of your nail and let that dry for another couple of minutes.  You're good to go.  Just to be fair, I paint with clear nail strengther - so it's pretty forgiving.  I still think these drops would help cut down the time you wait for sure.
cot: 1. Clues: Minor qualification about advertising claims, but strong positive indicators ('like it', 'will continue to use', 'super easy', helpful detailed instructions). 'That being said' reframes limitation as acceptable. 2. Reasoning: The reviewer acknowledges imperfection but clearly values the product's utility. The commitment to continued use and recommendation intent support positive classification despite 4-star rating. 3. Verdict: Positive sentiment with moderate-high confidence.

text:
Title: Great sunblock - a little greasy, but does the job
Review: Slightly oily (why I rated it 4 stars), but it is light. Needed to wait a bit before applying my foundation (to assure it was absorbed in). Works well, love that it is an SPF50 for my face. Lasted hours while I was outside (used this on a cruise where the sun was brutal). This bottle will last a long time as a little goes a long way.
cot: 1. Clues: The reviewer explicitly states 'why I rated it 4 stars' due to oiliness, but uses strong positive language like 'Works well,' 'love,' 'Lasted hours,' and 'Highly recommend' (implied by satisfaction). The minor complaint about oiliness is outweighed by multiple functional benefits. 2. Reasoning: The 4-star rating indicates predominantly positive sentiment with a minor reservation. The reviewer emphasizes effectiveness, longevity, and value—core satisfaction drivers. The oiliness issue is presented as manageable ('light,' 'wait a bit') rather than deal-breaking. 3. Verdict: The soft label should reflect strong positive sentiment with modest uncertainty from the single drawback.

text:

Review: Not easy to use. Ripped one trying to open it initially, I guess I didn’t zip it back and forth enough???  Did protect clothes.
cot: 1. Clues: 'Not as advertised,' 'Not easy to use,' 'Ripped one' (product failure); 'Did protect clothes' (one positive function); confused tone with '???' 2. Reasoning: The core claim 'Not as advertised' and immediate damage on first use dominate. The single positive 'did protect clothes' is minimal and past tense—function achieved but process failed. The questioning tone suggests user uncertainty about whether they or product are at fault, but damage occurred regardless. 3. Verdict: Strongly negative due to usability failure and damage.

'Super annoying' indicates strong emotional response. No redeeming qualities mentioned. 3. Verdict: Negative sentiment matching 1-star rating.

text:
Title: Revlon quality eyebrow liner
Review: Nice eyebrow pencil, but it is fairly thin and not the way I typically like a brow marker to be. Might work for others and I really like Revlon products so I am rating this as 4 stars. Color is really nice (soft brown) and it does cover well, just because it is thin I need to apply several times to cover fully. Probably will work well for others.
cot: 1. Clues: Personal preference mismatch ('not the way I typically like') balanced against brand loyalty, good color, adequate coverage, and explicit 4-star statement. 'Nice' and 'really like' indicate satisfaction. 2. Reasoning: The 4-star rating reflects that personal ergonomic preference doesn't prevent overall approval. Reviewer acknowledges subjectivity ('Might work for others', 'Probably will work well for others'). Brand affinity and color quality sustain positive assessment despite application inconvenience. 3. text:
Title: Didn't work for long
Review: The first one broke within about 2 weeks.  The replacement worked for a short time and now I get that dreaded message about it not being certified and doesn't charge my phone anymore.
cot: 1. Clues: 'broke within about 2 weeks', 'replacement worked for a short time', 'dreaded message', 'doesn't charge'; pattern of repeated failure. 2. Reasoning: Two consecutive failures indicate fundamental quality issues. 'Dreaded message' suggests ongoing frustration with known problem. No positive aspects mentioned; even the replacement failed. The temporal pattern (2 weeks, then 'short time') shows deteriorating reliability. 3. Verdict: Strongly negative sentiment matching the 1-star rating.

text:
Title: Dock extender - does not work
Review: Purchased the dock extender for my new iPhone 4S with an OtterBox Commuter case.  I have multiple radios with an iPhone dock.  With my previous iPhone 3GS with an OtterBox Commuter case, I could just dock it on the radio.  Once docked, I could then charge my iPhone and also play songs/books from the iPhone through the radio speakers.  Also, the radio controls would operate my iPhone.  Apparently with my new iPhone 4S/case combination, caused it not to dock snuggly and not work with my iPhone docking radios.  I purchased the dock extender cable in anticipation that all would work as it did previously.  The cable appears to make a good connection and the iPhone make a sound when I plug it into the cable.  Unfortunately, the iPhone will no longer play through the radio nor will any of the radio controls work with the iPhone.  I guess I will have to look at getting a different type of docking radio.  Also, I tried a different dock extender cable from another Amazon vendor and it also did not work.
cot: 1. Clues: Title 'does not work' explicit failure statement; detailed description of expected functionality from previous setup; 'not dock snuggly and not work' failure with new device; 'appears to make a good connection' false positive indicator; 'Unfortunately, the iPhone will no longer p' truncated but clearly negative outcome. 2. Reasoning: The reviewer carefully establishes baseline expectations from previous successful experience, then documents how this product fails to restore that functionality despite appearing to connect. The detailed technical context emphasizes the specific nature of the failure. The truncation cuts off but 'Unfortunately' signals clear negative resolution. 1-star rating confirms negative sentiment. 3. Verdict: Negative sentiment.

text:
Title: Great Air Flow .. with a 2 cent power button Review: Love this case .. roomy nice airflow lots of bays
But then they go and put a lousy toggle switch for a power button on top ?
Horrible placement, and switch doesn't work 1/2 time
Great case less the cheap power button .. had to replace with my own power button

Amazing a 5k+ high end build was brought to a halt by a cheap power button !
Come on Phantek !
cot: 1. Clues: Praise for case features like airflow and room. Severe criticism of power button quality and placement. Required modification to work. 2. Reasoning: High quality components are undermined by a critical cheap failure point. The experience is mixed between great design and poor execution on a key feature. 3. Verdict: The balance of excellent features and critical failure results in a neutral sentiment, matching the three-star rating.

text:
Title: It is awesome!!!
Review: This product was awesome and I would've kept it but it did not fit my Galaxy Buds2; Even though it implys that it will fit my type of case. It was too big for my earbuds case, unfortunately. I really liked it because it glows in the dark and was pink. Would've preferred a lavender or some sort of shade of purple that glowed in the dark though!
cot: 1. Clues: 'awesome' appears twice in title and text, 'really liked it', 'would've kept it' vs 'did not fit', 'too big', 'unfortunately', mismatch between title enthusiasm and return outcome. 2. Reasoning: The reviewer genuinely loves the product's features (glow-in-dark, color) but cannot use it due to sizing issue. The positive emotions about the product itself are strong, but the functional failure prevents satisfaction. This creates genuine ambivalence—happy with quality, unhappy with outcome. 3. Verdict: Neutral sentiment balancing strong product appreciation against complete usability failure.

text:
Title: An Okay Television
Review: Not a bad TV not a great TV. Needed for a bedroom where it's more used for company or background noise than for watching. That being said within the limited viewing angle the picture is reasonably good. For the money this suits our needs.
cot: 1. Clues: 'Okay', 'Not a bad TV not a great TV', 'suits our needs', 'reasonably good', 'limited viewing angle'. 2. Reasoning: The review explicitly states mediocrity ('not bad, not great') but contextualizes this as acceptable given the use case (secondary TV, background use). The product meets functional needs adequately for the price. The lukewarm language throughout indicates neither enthusiasm nor disappointment. 3. Verdict: Neutral sentiment.

text:
Title: When I say it works overnight, it does!  Deserves a 10 star rating!!!
Review: I really wish I had taken a before shot of my hands so you could see the difference this made.  I find that my hands are starting to become cracked and itchy because of both colder temps and that I am washing my hands a lot since the COVID crisis.  Since I received the items yesterday I immediately started using them because my hands were really suffering.  My hands were literally fixed overnight.  No more itching, no more redness, and they feel amazing.  I love that there is a day and night treatment.  Both work amazingly well and I would highly recommend to anyone looking to heal their poor tired hands.  If I could give it 10 stars, I would.  Thanks O'Keeffe's for your amazing products!!!
cot: 1. Clues: 'Deserves a 10 star rating', 'literally fixed overnight', 'No more itching, no more redness', 'feel amazing', 'work amazingly well', 'highly recommend', 'If I could give it 10 stars, I would', 'amazing products'. 2. Reasoning: The reviewer expresses extreme satisfaction with dramatic overnight results, uses superlative language throughout, explicitly states desire to give higher than maximum rating, and thanks the company. This is unambiguously enthusiastic. 3. Verdict: Strongly positive sentiment matching the 5-star rating.

text:
Title: Shampoo has almost ZERO lather but it’s moisturizing; fragrance OK; pump terrible to open Review: I ordered this shampoo and their [[ASIN:B0896V9PB1 combo pack]] at the same time and regretted it. I have short to medium fine curly hair and this left my hair conditioned but flat; I really dislike this. The fragrance is just okay. The current price of $39.57 is insane for this but I would not buy this at any price after trying this.

The shampoo almost has ZERO lather and feels like I am washing with a hand lotion. Their other shampoo was even worse. I did not even feel like this got my hair clean.

Also, it took me about 20 minutes of fiddling with the top (before I got in the shower), to open the pump. Once I got it open, I had to pump it about 9 times before the shampoo dispensed. I had high hopes for this shampoo but they have been dashed based on my results.
cot: 1. Clues: 'regretted it,' 'really dislike this,' 'insane for this,' 'would not buy this at any price,' 'ZERO lather,' 'did not even feel like this got my hair clean,' '20 minutes of fiddling.' Minor positives: 'moisturizing,' 'fragrance OK.' 2. Reasoning: Despite acknowledging some benefits (moisturizing), the reviewer expresses strong regret and multiple functional failures (no lather, doesn't feel clean, difficult packaging). The explicit 'would not buy at any price' and 'regretted' indicate strong negative sentiment. The 2-star rating confirms this. 3. Verdict: Negative - multiple significant product failures outweigh minor positives.

text:

The money. However, these specific socks are not as strong at the toe as other puma socks

Review: Like it but too tight and fit size good but need wide thanks

text:
Title: Large barely fragrant bath bombs w/zero performance; purple/gold ones turn the water almost black Review: There are eight individually wrapped bath bombs in this set. They are lightly fragrant but not really pleasingly so.

I dissolved one purple/gold bomb in close to hot water and there were no suds created, only a fizzing action. The water immediately looked almost black and glittery (due to the gold). Since I personally do not like the color rings caused by bath bombs in my bath, I use them in a foot bath. One bath bomb dissolves in less than five minutes in close to hot water. The fragrance was barely detectable. I personally would never want to bathe with these. I am glad, however, that the color did not adhere to my skin, my foot bath, or my towels.

The current price of $15.99 comes to $2 per lackluster bath bomb. I'll pass on these as they are one of the worst bath bombs sets I ever tried.
cot: 1. Clues: 'barely fragrant', 'not really pleasingly so', 'zero performance', 'no suds', 'almost black', 'would never want to bathe with these', 'glad...color did not adhere'. 2. Reasoning: Extensive criticism of core product functions (fragrance, suds, appearance, duration). The single positive note is a relief about something that didn't happen rather than an active benefit. Strong rejection of the product for intended use. 3. Verdict: Strongly negative sentiment.

text:
Title: Great for hiking
Review: I wore this on a 7 mile hike up and down a mountain in the spring in the south - it was very comfortable, I felt cool and stayed dry. I did cut the stitches between the thumb hole and the hem - they just hold the seam closed, the seam itself is sewn separately so this enlarges the opening without damaging the seam. I would purchase another.
cot: 1. Clues: Multiple positive descriptors ('very comfortable', 'cool', 'stayed dry'), explicit satisfaction statement ('would purchase another'), and a constructive modification suggestion rather than a complaint. 2. Reasoning: The reviewer demonstrates genuine enthusiasm through detailed positive experience sharing and clear repurchase intent. The minor modification mentioned is presented as a helpful tip, not a flaw. The 5-star rating confirms overwhelming satisfaction with functionality and comfort. 3. Verdict: Strongly positive sentiment with high confidence.

text:
Title: Seemed ok, but not worth it overall Review: These switches work fine for awhile, but once I started to introduce TIVO devices into my network, these switches were nothing but problems. Since I switched out these switches, my entire network has stabilized and been faster,

Overall I cannot recommend these green switches as they seem to have issues with certain devices.

In the end .. switch quality does matter
cot: 1. Clues: 'Seemed ok' (qualified positive), 'but not worth it overall', 'nothing but problems', 'cannot recommend', 'issues with certain devices'. 2. Reasoning: Initial impression was neutral-to-positive, but actual usage revealed critical compatibility failures. The reviewer explicitly states they replaced the product and saw improvement, confirming the product was the problem. Title acknowledges partial functionality but overall verdict is negative. 3. Verdict: Negative due to fundamental incompatibility issues despite initial appearance of working.

text:
Title: Really nice facial cleanser, great for sensitive and problem skin
Review: I have extremely sensitive, dry skin, exacerbated by some moderate psoriasis. I am always on the look out for skin products I can use which will not irritate my skin and will improve the overall appearance of my skin. When I saw this cleanser I wanted to try it and I am glad I did. First, I love the light (almost indistinguishable) fragrance and second I love how clean and fresh my skin feels after using it, removing all makeup. I have been using this twice daily exclusively for over 3 weeks and I can tell you that this is one of the finest products I have used on my face to date. I highly recommend!
cot: 1. Clues: The title uses positive descriptors with specific skin-type endorsement. The review establishes difficult skin conditions (sensitive, dry, psoriasis) to heighten the achievement of satisfaction. Multiple 'love' statements, explicit recommendation, and superlative ranking ('one of the finest products') demonstrate enthusiasm. The sustained usage ('twice daily exclusively for over 3 weeks') validates real-world testing. 2. Reasoning: The reviewer positions themselves as a discriminating, difficult-to-satisfy customer whose needs are exceptionally met. The specific benefits (non-irritating, makeup removal, clean feeling) address claimed needs. The temporal element (3 weeks of exclusive use) shows commitment beyond initial impression. The enthusiastic conclusion with exclamation mark confirms genuine satisfaction. 3. Verdict: Strongly positive sentiment with high confidence.

text:
Title: works, just a bulky applicator.
Review: the application was awkward and because it was effetely a giant rounded sponge, the color covered more area that I wanted.  However it stayed on and washed away in the next shampoo wash.  No odor and I wouldn't try sleep with it as it did smear on my hand after a workout - not sure how much you sweat when you sleep.  i think there are better applicators out there.
cot: 1. Clues: Negative: 'awkward,' 'giant rounded sponge,' covered 'more area that I wanted,' smearing concern, 'better applicators out there.' Positive: 'works,' 'stayed on,' proper removal ('washed away'), 'No odor.' 2. Reasoning: Functional success (color staying, no odor, removable) is undermined by poor user experience (awkward application, imprecision, potential transfer). The reviewer's concluding comparison to alternatives confirms mediocrity. The 3-star rating reflects adequate but unimpressive performance—meets basic needs but with notable friction. 3. Verdict: Neutral sentiment with negative lean on usability.

text:
Title: Thin, not as soft as other shirts of this type, true to size; tag is annoying Review: I got this for me to wear as an undershirt. It is a nice shirt but is thin. It is imported from Argentina and is made of 100% polyester. The tags on the internal left side of the shirt are super annoying. I had to cut them out as they are about 6 inches in length and kept bothering me. Other that the lack of softness and annoying tags, this is a nice shirt.

I wash this in a lingerie bag and hang dry it.
cot: 1. Clues: 'nice shirt but is thin', 'not as soft', 'super annoying', 'lack of softness' list negatives; 'nice shirt', 'true to size', and continued use (care instructions) show acceptance. 2. Reasoning: Multiple specific complaints about comfort and construction are partially offset by functional adequacy. The 3-star rating reflects this 'acceptable but flawed' assessment—usable after modifications (cutting tags) but not ideal. 3. Verdict: Neutral sentiment with notable drawbacks.

text:
Title: OK if you're a Roman Soldier
Review: Looks like a copper-colored hat. Sucks.

The Roman soldier reference implies the hat looks like costume armor rather than normal headwear. 3. Verdict: Strongly negative sentiment, matching 2-star rating.

text:
Title: I recommend to anyone with eczema or any sensitive skin issues!
Review: I have eczema and I am always looking for items that will not impact my skin. Body washes have tended to be very drying on my skin, causing it to peel and itch after using, so I had stopped using it. I was anxious to see how Dove did, since I do rely on them for alot of my skin needs. I can say that this body wash is great, my skin comes out soft and clean after using and I have no negative side effects. I now keep one in my shower and use this daily and I absolutely love it! Highly recommend!
cot: 1. Clues: 'great', 'soft and clean', 'no negative side effects', 'absolutely love it', 'Highly recommend' are unambiguous positive markers. Past struggles with other products heighten appreciation. 2. Reasoning: Personal health problem solved, daily use commitment, emotional language ('love'), and strong recommendation all point to exceptional satisfaction. 3. Verdict: Strongly positive sentiment matching 5-star rating.

text:
Title: Works well
Review: Stays on well and the color is perfect. No tugging to put it on.

All mentioned attributes are positive: longevity, color accuracy, and ease of application. No criticism or reservations expressed. 3. Verdict: Positive sentiment.

text:
Title: Too dark for lite fair coloring. Review: This is too dark a color for my fair coloring. I tried just a small amount on my two fingers, and a little moisturizer also.
This did not spread creamy on my cheeks, and I thought my skin is a bit oily!

For me I looked like a little old lady with too much blush on with white skin.

Just not for me...
Although I think if you have darker color skin this would look good.
I usually use a pink color of blush..
cot: 1. Clues: 'Too dark', 'did not spread creamy', 'looked like a little old lady' (negative self-image), 'Just not for me' (neutral dismissal), 'would look good' on others (balanced perspective). 2. text:
Title: This is the color of wet cotton candy
Review: This is not the pale pink that's on the package. It's more dark pink. I thought it would be a light cotton candy pink but this is the color of the cotton candy after it gets wet. You know that deep pink cotton candy gets when its wet? Its that exact color. They lie and now I have to live with it. If you dont want a dark pink dont buy this.
cot: 1. Clues: 'not the pale pink that's on the package', 'They lie' (accusation of deception), 'now I have to live with it' (resigned frustration), explicit warning to others. 2. The 'have to live with it' suggests purchase regret without return option. However, the 2-star rating (not 1) may indicate the product is usable just not as described. 3. When worn they looked way too tight and I think they are made way too thin even maybe for teenagers not fit for adult sizes.
